digest.top_line: DOREMY: 5yr LRFS 97.4% with de-escalated 36Gy/18fx preop RT in myxoid liposarcoma, supports dropping below the standard 50Gy.
digest.tldr: Sarcoma carries the day's actionable RT signal: DOREMY holds 97.4% 5yr LRFS after cutting preop dose to 36Gy/18fx in myxoid liposarcoma (single-arm, 66mo f/u). In prostate, REVELUTION supplies a coronary-plaque substrate favoring relugolix over leuprolide in men on pelvic RT + ADT (surrogate endpoint, N=94).

sites.intro: MLS radiosensitivity underwrites the sub-50Gy preop dose question DOREMY tests.

studies.name: DOREMY
studies.tldr: 5yr LRFS 97.4% after de-escalated 36Gy/18fx preop RT (vs standard 50Gy) in myxoid liposarcoma; wound complications 21%.

details.text: 📊 Long-term outcomes at 5 years (single arm, N=90)

details: 🔍 Prospective single-group phase 2, 9 tertiary sarcoma centers (EU + US), N=90, median f/u 66.4mo
🔍 Preop RT 36Gy/18fx (2Gy daily), de-escalated from the standard 50Gy/25fx, then resection
📊 Late toxicity low: G2 15% (13 pts), G3 3% (3 pts)
🔗 MLS is exquisitely radiosensitive; this is the rationale for testing a lower dose vs standard 50Gy
⚠️ Single-arm, nonrandomized: no direct comparator, non-inferiority vs 50Gy not formally tested
🔍 Surgery omitted in 3 pts (3%) due to intercurrent metastatic disease

verdict.rationale: Single-arm nonrandomized phase 2; no randomised comparator vs standard 50Gy, so non-inferiority untested. Maturity gate caps single-arm at early-signal despite 66-mo follow-up.
verdict.audience: Localized translocation-confirmed MLS of trunk or extremity, mean age 47

studies.significance: The RT read is de-escalation: 36Gy/18fx (vs standard 50Gy/25fx) held 5yr LRFS at 97.4% with 21% wound complications in translocation-confirmed trunk/extremity MLS. It moves the preop-dose decision toward de-escalation for this radiosensitive histology, though without a randomised 50Gy comparator this rests on shared decision-making, not proven non-inferiority.
studies.monday_clinic: In localized translocation-confirmed myxoid liposarcoma of the trunk or extremity going to preoperative RT plus resection, this supports a reduced 36Gy preop dose; it does not extend to retroperitoneal sites or metastatic disease.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Prospective **single-group phase 2** nonrandomized trial, 9 tertiary sarcoma centers (EU + US), **N=90**, enrolled 2010-2020. Median follow-up **66.4 mo** (IQR 48.8-87.5).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Adults with biopsy-proven, **translocation-confirmed localized MLS of trunk or extremity**. Mean age 47, 56% male.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **36 Gy in 18 daily 2-Gy fractions** preoperatively (vs standard **50 Gy/25 fx**), delivered per protocol in all 90 pts, then resection.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Wound complications **21%** (18/90), **16%** (14) needing intervention. Late toxicity low: **G2 15%** (13 pts), **G3 3%** (3 pts).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Standard preop soft-tissue-sarcoma dose is **50 Gy/25 fx**; MLS is exquisitely radiosensitive. Reduced **36 Gy** preserves local control here, but there is **no randomised 50 Gy comparator**.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents localized translocation-confirmed myxoid liposarcoma of the trunk or extremity going to preop RT plus resection. Does not represent retroperitoneal MLS or metastatic disease.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Single-arm, nonrandomized**; no formal non-inferiority test vs 50 Gy. Authors note a phase 3 is impractical for this rare cancer.

open_questions: Durability of local control beyond 5 years
Whether dose can be reduced further below 36 Gy

Exclusion Criteria:

* Prior radiotherapy to the target area
*…
related_trials.answers_question: Durability of local control beyond 5 years
related_trials.relevance_phrase: prospective MLS registry, 36 vs 50Gy local control

sites.intro: Not an efficacy signal: the read here is cardiovascular safety of the ADT agent chosen alongside pelvic RT.

studies.name: REVELUTION
studies.tldr: Leuprolide added 68.9 mm³ more total coronary plaque than relugolix at 12mo (adjusted), in men on pelvic RT + ADT.
details: 🔍 Single-institution open-label RCT, 4 Emory centers, N=94 non-metastatic prostate CA, all on pelvic RT ± pelvic nodes
🔍 ADT-eligible pts randomized 1:1 relugolix vs leuprolide; parallel prospective RT-alone cohort (no ADT) as control
🔍 1° EP: 12-mo change in total coronary plaque volume on serial CCTA (HeartFlow automated quant, reads blinded to arm)
💊 Relugolix 360mg load → 120mg/day; leuprolide 3-mo depots; ADT ≥6mo, randomization stratified by ASCVD 10-yr risk

details.text: 📊 12-mo coronary plaque change, leuprolide vs relugolix

details: 🔗 vs HERO (2020): HERO showed lower MACE with relugolix but mechanism unclear (similar T-suppression); REVELUTION supplies a coronary-plaque substrate
⚠️ Surrogate imaging endpoint (plaque volume), not clinical MACE; N=94, single institution, 12-mo f/u
⚠️ Open-label; per-arm randomized counts not reported in source

verdict.rationale: Small (N=94) single-institution open-label RCT; surrogate imaging endpoint (coronary plaque volume), not clinical MACE; 12-mo follow-up. Mechanistically supports HERO's relugolix signal.
verdict.audience: Intermediate/high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer, pelvic RT + ADT

studies.significance: Non-calcified plaque, the MACE-predictive subtype, drove the 68.9 mm³ adjusted total-plaque difference; calcified and low-attenuation plaque didn't differ. For the radonc prescribing ADT alongside pelvic RT in intermediate/high-risk disease, this is a mechanistic reason to favor relugolix over leuprolide in cardiovascularly at-risk men.
studies.monday_clinic: For the intermediate/high-risk man receiving ADT with pelvic RT who carries elevated ASCVD risk, this supports relugolix over leuprolide; it does not extend to lower-risk RT-alone pts or to clinical cardiac events.

primary_endpoint.name: Change in total coronary plaque volume
primary_endpoint.stat_value: 68.9 mm³ more with leuprolide vs relugolix
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: Adjusted for age, statin, baseline plaque; crude 56 vs 25 mm³; no CI/p in source

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Single-institution open-label RCT**, 4 Emory-affiliated centers, enrolled June 2020 to 2024. 94 men with non-metastatic prostate cancer; ADT-eligible pts randomized 1:1 to relugolix vs leuprolide, with a **parallel prospective RT-alone cohort (no ADT)** as control.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **Intermediate and high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer.** ADT-arm randomization stratified by **ASCVD 10-year risk score**; the RT-alone control drawn from lower-risk pts eligible for definitive RT without planned ADT.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Relugolix **360 mg load → 120 mg daily**; leuprolide **3-month depots**. ADT ≥6 months, duration by cancer risk tier.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: All pts received **pelvic radiotherapy to the prostate with or without pelvic lymph nodes**. Dose, fractionation, and technique not reported in source.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: 12-month change in total coronary plaque volume** on serial CCTA (HeartFlow automated quantification, reads blinded to arm). Secondary: non-calcified, calcified, low-attenuation plaque subtypes. ANCOVA adjusted for age, statin use, baseline plaque.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: **HERO (2020)** showed relugolix lowered MACE vs leuprolide, but the mechanism was unclear given similar testosterone suppression. REVELUTION offers a substrate: **greater non-calcified coronary plaque progression on leuprolide**.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents intermediate/high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer on pelvic RT plus ADT. Does not represent metastatic disease or men managed without ADT.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Single institution, N=94, open-label. **Surrogate imaging endpoint** (coronary plaque volume), not clinical MACE; 12-month follow-up. Per-arm randomized counts not reported in source.

open_questions: Whether reduced plaque progression translates to fewer clinical cardiac events
Durability of the plaque difference beyond 12 months

related_trials.brief_title: Effects of Relugolix vs Leuprolide on Cardiac Function in Patients With Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial compares the effect of relugolix to leuprolide on cardiac function and performance in patients with prostate cancer. Androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) has been a key component for the treatment of advanced prostate cancer for decades. The term androgen deprivation therapy means lowering a man's testosterone. related_trials.brief_summary: This phase III/IV trial compares the impact of leuprolide and abiraterone acetate (AA) versus relugolix and AA on the heart in hormone-naive patients with advanced prostate cancer receiving pelvic radiation therapy. Leuprolide is in a class of medications called gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonists (GNRHa). papers.title: Dose Reduction of Preoperative Radiotherapy in Myxoid Liposarcoma: The Phase 2 DOREMY Nonrandomized Clinical Trial.
papers.abstract:
IMPORTANCE: Prospective data from 2 phase 2 trials showed favorable wound complication rates and promising local control after a reduced preoperative radiotherapy dose for myxoid liposarcoma (MLS). However, long-term follow-up data are currently lacking.

OBJECTIVE: To determine the efficacy and toxicity profile of a reduced preoperative radiotherapy dose in patients with MLS with long-term follow-up.

DESIGN, SETTING, AND PARTICIPANTS: The Dose Reduction of Preoperative Radiotherapy in Myxoid Liposarcoma (DOREMY) trial is a prospective, single-group, phase 2 nonrandomized clinical trial conducted in 9 tertiary sarcoma centers in Europe and the US. Eligible patients were adults with biopsy-proven and translocation-confirmed localized MLS of the trunk or extremity who were enrolled from November 24, 2010, to May 14, 2020. Data were analyzed from January to December 2025.

INTERVENTION: Preoperative radiotherapy to a reduced dose of 36 Gy in once-daily 2-Gy fractions followed by resection.

MAIN OUTCOMES AND MEASURES: Long-term local recurrence-free survival, progression-free survival, disease-specific survival, overall survival, and late toxic effects.

RESULTS: Ninety patients (mean [SD] age, 47 [13.1] years; 50 [56%] male) were included and followed up for a median (IQR) of 66.4 (48.8-87.5) months. Preoperative radiotherapy was delivered according to protocol in all patients. Surgery was not performed in 3 patients (3%) due to intercurrent metastatic disease. Local recurrence-free survival, progression-free survival, disease-specific survival, and overall survival rates at 5 years were 97.4% (95% CI, 93.9%-100%), 81.0% (95% CI, 72.6%-89.4%), 89.5% (95% CI, 82.6%-96.4%), and 88.5% (95% CI, 81.2%-95.8%), respectively. In total, 18 patients (21%) experienced a wound complication, and 14 (16%) required intervention. Any grade 2 or grade 3 late toxic effects were seen among 13 patients (15%) and 3 patients (3%), respectively.

CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE: This long-term analysis of the DOREMY nonrandomized clinical trial demonstrated excellent local control and a favorable toxicity profile following dose reduction of preoperative radiotherapy in patients with MLS. These compelling phase 2 findings support adoption of this regimen as an appropriate treatment option through shared decision-making with the patient, given the impracticality of conducting a phase 3 trial for a rare cancer.

TRIAL REGISTRATION: ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT02106312.
